cmd.purpose: Organisational root for all deployable binary entrypoints (server, billing-worker, balance-worker, sink-worker, notification-service, jobs, benthos-collector). Each child is a thin Wire+main.go launcher that loads Viper config, calls a Wire-generated initializeApplication, runs migrations, and starts an oklog/run.Group; all business logic lives in openmeter/* or app/common.

patterns.name: Wire-only entrypoint
patterns.description: Each binary contains only wire.go (//go:build wireinject + wire.Build over app/common provider sets), wire_gen.go (generated), main.go, and version.go. No domain logic or shared state lives here.
patterns.example: cmd/sink-worker/wire.go: wire.Build(common.NewSinkWorker, ...); wire_gen.go generated by `make generate`.

patterns.name: Lifecycle via oklog/run.Group
patterns.description: Long-running actors (HTTP server, Kafka consumer, telemetry server, sink loop) are added to run.Group via common.Runner or explicit run.Add; no goroutine is spawned outside run.Group so graceful shutdown is preserved.

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before Run
patterns.description: Provisioning (EnsureBusinessAccounts, SandboxProvisioner, initNamespace, default billing profile) runs after app.Migrate(ctx) but before the run.Group starts consuming events, each guarded by error handling + os.Exit(1).

patterns.name: Ordered namespace handler registration (server only)
patterns.description: cmd/server is the only binary that registers namespace.Handler implementations; Ledger then KafkaIngest must be registered via manager.RegisterHandler before initNamespace so the default namespace receives every handler's provisioning.
patterns.example: cmd/server/main.go: register ledger handler, register kafkaIngest handler, then initNamespace(ctx, app.NamespaceManager).

patterns.name: Binary identity via common.NewMetadata + ldflags version
patterns.description: Each binary's version.go declares `var version = "unknown"` overridden by ldflags (runtime/debug fallback) and a metadata() function feeding OTel service.name and log fields.

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written Wire provider graph for the binary (//go:build wireinject + wire.Build).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never remove the //go:build wireinject tag; adding a domain service requires adding its app/common provider set here and regenerating.

key_file_guides.role: Generated DI assembly produced by `go generate`/`make generate` from wire.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Manual edits are overwritten on next generate; regenerate after any wire.go or constructor signature change.

key_file_guides.role: Registers namespace handlers, provisions default namespace + billing profile, builds router.Config from all ~40 services, runs a five-actor run.Group (API, telemetry, Kafka producer, NotificationEventHandler goroutine, termination checker).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Handler order is load-bearing (Ledger before KafkaIngest, both before initNamespace); uses common.WatermillNoPublisher because the server does not consume topics.

key_file_guides.role: Provisions ledger business accounts and sandbox app, wires AppRegistry (Sandbox/Stripe/CustomInvoicing), then starts the Watermill consumer loop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EnsureBusinessAccounts and SandboxProvisioner must complete before run.Group.Run(); skipping EnsureBusinessAccounts leaves the ledger without business accounts.

key_file_guides.role: Wire assembles services, then main.go manually constructs the Watermill Kafka subscriber and notification consumer and runs them in run.Group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep subscriber construction in main.go so the consumer group name comes from config at startup; use app.EventPublisher.Marshaler() (not app.MessagePublisher) for deserialization.

key_file_guides.role: Loads + Validate()s Viper config, Wire-inits, runs Sink + TelemetryServer actors with reverse shutdown order and unconditional cleanup defer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All sink logic lives in openmeter/sink; never construct sink.Sink/streaming.Connector directly here; use root ctx not context.Background() in actors.

key_file_guides.role: Cobra CLI: PersistentPreRunE wires the whole app once into internal.App package-level globals; sub-commands source services from there.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never construct domain services inline in RunE; always cmd.Context() not context.Background(); nil-guard optional app services; ledger backfill intentionally bypasses Wire for concrete adapters.

key_file_guides.role: Thin launcher that blank-imports collector plugin packages then calls service.RunCLI; no Wire DI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register new collector plugins via blank import here; do not add Wire or domain service instantiation.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to main.go instead of openmeter/* or app/common.
Manually editing wire_gen.go instead of regenerating via `make generate`.
Registering a namespace.Handler after initNamespace in cmd/server, so the default namespace misses that handler's provisioning.
Calling EnsureBusinessAccounts/SandboxProvisioner after Run(), or spawning a goroutine outside run.Group.
Introducing context.Background()/context.TODO() anywhere other than the root context at program start in main().

decisions.decision: All business logic lives in openmeter/* or app/common; cmd/* is wiring and lifecycle only.
decisions.rationale: Keeps domain packages independently testable and reusable across binaries without importing cmd/*.

decisions.decision: Each binary owns its own Wire provider graph in wire.go rather than sharing one graph.
decisions.rationale: Different binaries need different provider subsets; separate graphs prevent unintended coupling and reduce binary size.

decisions.decision: cmd/notification-service constructs its Watermill subscriber in main.go, not in Wire.
decisions.rationale: The consumer group name must come from runtime config at startup, which is unavailable during Wire's compile-time graph resolution.

code_examples.scenario: Wire provider declaration for a new binary entrypoint

collector.purpose: Separate Go module (own go.mod, replace github.com/openmeterio/openmeter => ../) housing the Benthos/Redpanda Connect collector: the custom plugin surface (collector/benthos) activated by blank imports from cmd/benthos-collector, plus a local docker-compose demo (collector/quickstart). It is the production-ready ingestion pipeline that collects, transforms, buffers, and reliably delivers usage events into OpenMeter; no openmeter domain Wire graph is instantiated here.

patterns.name: Blank-import plugin activation
patterns.description: Benthos plugins must register in init() before process start; the launcher imports collector/benthos sub-packages as _ to trigger registration. Plugins are never wired by calling their constructors.

patterns.name: Sub-package per concern
patterns.description: collector/benthos splits into input/, bloblang/, internal/ (pure utils), services/ (leader-election), and presets/ (pure YAML), each owning exactly one concern; cross-cutting utilities go only to internal/.
patterns.example: collector/benthos/internal/shutdown/signaller.go is a utility, not a plugin.

patterns.name: Leader-election gate on replicated work
patterns.description: Work that must not run on every replica checks leaderelection.IsLeader(res); leader state is stored in service.Resources generic map and an absent key defaults to true (safe single-replica deployment).
patterns.example: if !leaderelection.IsLeader(res) { return nil } // skip on non-leader replicas

patterns.name: Env-var substitution in YAML presets
patterns.description: All preset and quickstart YAML uses ${VAR:default} interpolation; no endpoints, URLs, or tokens are hardcoded.

key_file_guides.role: Thin launcher: blank-imports plugin packages then calls service.RunCLI with a cancellable root context and leader-election CLI options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add no pipeline logic here; register plugins via blank import; keep the cancellable context (do not pass a bare context.Background() to RunCLI).

key_file_guides.role: Leader-election state in service.Resources generic map with IsLeader(res) helper (absent-key=true default).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Read leadership only via IsLeader(res), never res.GetGeneric on IsLeaderKey, or the single-replica default is lost.

key_file_guides.role: Side-effect-only package registering bloblang functions in init(); exports nothing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not export symbols or place business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Custom Benthos input plugins registered in init().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Return transient per-resource failures by logging and skipping; a returned error from ReadBatch halts the whole pipeline.

key_file_guides.role: Local demo stack that includes ../../quickstart/docker-compose.yaml rather than duplicating service definitions; uses in-memory dedupe (not Redis).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use the custom openmeter output plugin (not generic http_client); keep debug_endpoints off outside local dev.

key_file_guides.role: Separate module pinning Redpanda benthos/connect and a large transitive dependency set; replaces openmeter with the parent repo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Built as a separate Docker image (benthos-collector.Dockerfile, CGO_ENABLED=0); dependency bumps are isolated from the root module.

anti_patterns: Registering a plugin outside init() — Benthos requires init-time registration before process start.
Adding business logic or plugin registrations to internal/ — it is a pure utilities layer.
Reading IsLeaderKey directly via res.GetGeneric instead of leaderelection.IsLeader(res), missing the absent-key=true default.
Exporting symbols from collector/benthos/bloblang — it is side-effect-only and must export nothing.
Hardcoding OPENMETER_URL/OPENMETER_TOKEN instead of ${VAR:default} substitution, or wiring an openmeter Wire graph into the collector.

decisions.decision: Sub-package per concern (input, bloblang, internal, services, presets) rather than a flat package.
decisions.rationale: Benthos plugin types have distinct registration and lifecycle semantics; separation prevents accidental cross-registration and clarifies what cmd/benthos-collector must blank-import.

decisions.decision: No Wire DI or openmeter domain service instantiation in collector/.
decisions.rationale: The Benthos framework provides its own component lifecycle; injecting openmeter Wire graphs would couple the collector to the full service dependency tree unnecessarily.

decisions.decision: Collector is its own Go module and Docker image.
decisions.rationale: Isolates the heavy Redpanda Connect dependency tree (CGO_ENABLED=0 build) from the main module's build and dependency graph.

code_examples.scenario: Launcher blank-imports plugins then runs the Benthos CLI

deploy.purpose: Kubernetes deployment artefacts for OpenMeter: two independently versioned, self-contained Helm charts (openmeter, benthos-collector) published to the GHCR OCI registry, plus a local kind cluster config. deploy/charts owns only the shared helm-docs generation pipeline consumed by both charts; no shared manifests, CRDs, or cluster-scoped RBAC live at this level.

patterns.name: helm-docs via shared template
patterns.description: Both chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from deploy/charts/template.md plus each chart's values.yaml comments; READMEs are never hand-edited.
patterns.example: make -C deploy/charts docs  # regenerates both chart READMEs

patterns.name: OCI Helm registry distribution
patterns.description: Charts are packaged and pushed to ghcr.io OCI registry on release tags rather than a traditional index-based Helm repo.

patterns.name: Enabled-guard + configmap override for optional infrastructure
patterns.description: Optional features (bundled Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis) use an enabled: bool guard in resource templates AND a matching override block in configmap.yaml so disabled services inject no config.
patterns.example: {{- if .Values.kafka.enabled }}...{{- end }} paired with the kafka section in configmap.yaml

patterns.name: Self-contained child charts
patterns.description: Each chart bundles everything it needs; all OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart, aligned to Chart.yaml appVersion.
patterns.example: deploy/charts/openmeter/values.yaml image tag aligned with Chart.yaml appVersion.

key_file_guides.role: Drives helm-docs README generation for both charts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a third chart requires a new README.tmpl.md and an update to this docs target.

key_file_guides.role: Shared helm-docs template consumed by both child charts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changes affect both READMEs; keep it generic with no chart-specific content.

key_file_guides.role: Renders OpenMeter config.yaml from Helm values; all optional infrastructure blocks need enabled guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A missing enabled guard injects config for disabled services.

key_file_guides.role: Local kind cluster config with ingress-ready node label and 80/443 port mappings.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Port mappings must match the chart service/ingress defaults to avoid local routing mismatches.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs and edits are overwritten on next `make docs`.
Adding a third chart without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and Makefile docs update.
Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained.
Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag in values.yaml.
Adding optional infrastructure without both an enabled guard in the template AND a matching override block in configmap.yaml.

decisions.decision: Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart.
decisions.rationale: Avoids README format drift between the two charts; single edit point for documentation structure.

decisions.decision: OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index.
decisions.rationale: OCI registries provide content-addressable artefact storage and align with the container image distribution model already used for application images.

decisions.decision: All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart.
decisions.rationale: The multi-stage Dockerfile produces all binaries from one image, so a single tag keeps deployed components version-consistent.

code_examples.scenario: Regenerate chart docs and package a chart for OCI release
code_examples.code:
make -C deploy/charts docs
helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version $VERSION --destination build/helm
helm push build/helm/openmeter-$VERSION.tgz oci://ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts

tools.purpose: Operational boundary between schema authorship and runtime startup. tools/migrate owns the Atlas-generated SQL migration chain, the golang-migrate runtime wrapper applied at binary startup, and the viewgen sub-tool that generates ClickHouse view DDL outside Atlas; wait-for-compose.sh is a CI readiness gate. Primary constraint: migrations/ SQL and atlas.sum are Atlas-owned and must never be hand-edited.

patterns.name: golang-migrate over embedded Atlas SQL
patterns.description: Migrations are embedded and applied at startup via golang-migrate (migrate.go), not the Atlas CLI; OMMigrationsConfig is the only config input (DB URL, migration table name).

patterns.name: SourceWrapper for migration:ignore filtering
patterns.description: fs.go wraps the embedded migration filesystem and strips files tagged migration:ignore so Atlas lint artefacts do not confuse golang-migrate.
patterns.example: migrate.New("iofs://migrations", db) uses the SourceWrapper to drop non-SQL files; filterErrNoChange swallows migrate.ErrNoChange.

patterns.name: Runner/stops migration tests on raw *sql.DB
patterns.description: migrate_test.go drives a table of stops {version, action} validating schema shape on raw *sql.DB, with purgeDB between down and up passes to assert idempotence.
patterns.example: stops := []migrationStop{{version: 20, action: func(db *sql.DB) error { /* assert schema */ }}}

patterns.name: viewgen for ClickHouse view DDL with parity test
patterns.description: tools/migrate/cmd/viewgen generates view SQL from Ent view schemas (Atlas does not diff ent.View); view_parity_test.go fails if views.sql diverges from viewgen output.

patterns.name: wait-for-compose polling gate
patterns.description: wait-for-compose.sh polls docker inspect health/state for listed services (60 attempts x 2s) before CI test steps run, falling back to state-based checks for containers without healthchecks.

key_file_guides.role: Runtime migration wrapper: embeds the migrations FS, applies via golang-migrate, exposes Migrate(ctx, db) used at binary startup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not call Atlas at runtime; OMMigrationsConfig is the only config input.

key_file_guides.role: SourceWrapper stripping migration:ignore files from the embedded FS before golang-migrate sees them.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any non-.up.sql/.down.sql file added to migrations/ must be tagged migration:ignore or it will confuse golang-migrate.

key_file_guides.role: Stop tests verifying schema shape at specific migration versions using raw *sql.DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use raw *sql.DB, not Ent (Ent only understands the current schema); call purgeDB between up and down passes.

key_file_guides.role: Asserts views.sql matches what viewgen would generate from the current Ent schema.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When ent.View schema changes, run viewgen and commit the updated views.sql or this test fails.

key_file_guides.role: Standalone tool reading Ent schema paths and writing ClickHouse view DDL to views.sql.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not import app/common or openmeter/ domain packages — it needs only Ent schema paths.

key_file_guides.role: Generated ClickHouse view DDL applied as an explicit SQL migration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated, not hand-edited; run `make generate-view-sql` to regenerate.

key_file_guides.role: CI readiness gate blocking until listed docker compose services are healthy or running.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Exits 1 on unhealthy state and times out after ~120s (60 x 2s); standalone bash so it runs before any Go binary is available.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing any file in tools/migrate/migrations/ — Atlas owns the chain and atlas.sum fails CI validation.
Calling `atlas migrate diff` expecting it to pick up a new ent.View schema — Atlas does not diff views; use viewgen.
Using Ent or any ORM inside migration stop-test actions — raw *sql.DB is required because the ORM only understands the current schema version.
Adding a migration stop test outside the runner/stops pattern — it skips the purgeDB/down/up cycle and can leave the DB dirty for other tests.
Hand-editing tools/migrate/views.sql — it is generated; run `make generate-view-sql`.

decisions.decision: golang-migrate wraps Atlas-generated SQL rather than using the Atlas CLI at runtime.
decisions.rationale: Atlas is a dev/CI tool; golang-migrate gives a stable embedded Go runtime for applying migrations at binary startup with no Atlas binary dependency.

decisions.decision: viewgen is a separate tool from Atlas migration generation.
decisions.rationale: Atlas does not diff ent.View schemas into SQL migrations; ClickHouse view DDL requires custom generation applied as an explicit SQL migration.

decisions.decision: wait-for-compose.sh is a standalone bash script rather than a Go helper.
decisions.rationale: It must run before any Go binary is available; bash docker-inspect polling has no runtime dependencies.

code_examples.scenario: Applying embedded migrations at binary startup
